steps.title: Substitute y = 17 into both equations
steps.content:
Since the intersection point is $(q, 17)$, we know $y = 17$ at this point. Substitute $y = 17$ into both original equations:

Equation 1: $-x - w(17) = -249$ becomes $-x - 17w = -249$
Equation 2: $2x - w(17) = 39$ becomes $2x - 17w = 39$

Now we have a system of two equations in two unknowns ($x$ and $w$).

steps.title: Subtract equations to eliminate w
steps.content:
Subtract equation 1 from equation 2 to eliminate the $w$ term:
$$(2x - 17w) - (-x - 17w) = 39 - (-249)$$
$$3x = 288$$
$$x = 96$$
So the x-coordinate of the intersection point is $q = 96$.
